rationale: The research question directly matches the bounded CUDA memory-hierarchy probe family. This configuration stays within the single-GPU scope, uses fixed launch parameters and seed for determinism, and is aggressive enough on working-set size and stride to make the locality hypothesis falsifiable within the round budget.
topic: explore memory hierarchy effects with strided global memory access

rationale: This is the bounded follow-up requested by the latest workflow proposal: a matched cache-friendly control run against the accepted 2048 KB, 128-byte-stride measurement. It stays within the real CUDA memory-hierarchy probe family, preserves deterministic controls, uses identical launch parameters for direct comparison, and fits the 120-second round budget.
topic: Run a matched cache-friendly control for the latest 2048 KB strided CUDA memory probe to test whether the observed bandwidth is due to memory-hierarchy locality rather than fixed launch configuration.

rationale: This follow-up uses the real CUDA memory-hierarchy probe and changes exactly one axis, `working_set_kb`, while holding constant the prior hostile-stride configuration from `spec_3626106571cf` (`stride_bytes=128`, `blocks=80`, `threads_per_block=128`, `iterations=20000`). It is bounded, single-GPU, deterministic, and fits the 120-second budget while providing a falsifiable capacity-sensitive comparison.
topic: Does reducing the working set to 256 KB, while holding the 128-byte stride and launch parameters fixed, materially raise effective bandwidth relative to the accepted 2048 KB hostile-stride CUDA probe?

rationale: Complete the minimal 2x2 matrix for axes `working_set_kb` in {2048, 256} and `stride_bytes` in {128, 4} using the real CUDA probe. Already measured cells are spec_3626106571cf (2048 KB, 128 B), spec_ea7294e5fd1a (2048 KB, 4 B), and spec_d89a9f30c9b4 (256 KB, 128 B). This spec fills the single missing matched-control cell (256 KB, 4 B) while preserving single-GPU scope, deterministic controls, benchmark family, and the 120-second round budget.
topic: Does the missing matched-control cell at 256 KB working set and 4-byte stride show substantially higher effective bandwidth than the 256 KB, 128-byte hostile-stride case under the same CUDA memory probe launch parameters?

rationale: The 2x2 corner matrix is already complete and verified. A single midpoint measurement on the 128-byte-stride axis is the smallest new real-CUDA experiment that tests whether the observed working-set sensitivity is an endpoint artifact or a directional trend, while preserving the same deterministic controls, single-GPU scope, and round budget.
topic: At fixed 128-byte stride, does an intermediate 1024 KB working set produce bandwidth between the verified 256 KB and 2048 KB hostile-stride endpoints under the same CUDA memory probe parameters?
